name: Avenc de Na Pola
starting_point: C/ Son Duran. Vehicle can be parked beside a small plaza.
best_season: Any, as it is very close to the town of Alaró.
equipment: 1 rope of 30 m for the pit, 1 rope of 40 m for the rest, 1 bolt, 5 carabiners, slings, and several protectors.
description: A 41-meter deep predominantly vertical pit near Alaró with beautifully decorated galleries and chambers. After a 21-meter aerial descent, visitors can explore the Pellet Gallery (a raptor roost), the Cut Chamber with impressive lance-shaped stalactites up to 2 meters long, and the Window Chamber with abundant formations. The deepest point is the small Bones Chamber at -41 m, floored with red clay. The pit has unfortunately been used for disposing of dogs.
full_description:
DESCRIPTION OF THE CAVITY

If we want direct access to the pit — that is, if we do not want to set up fractionations — the branch of a holm oak can serve us to set up the head of the installation, and we can secure it at some of the spits found near it.

After a 21 m aerial descent — close to the wall, which we can reach at many points — we will find ourselves at the base of a debris cone of approximately 4 m in depth where multiple bone remains of dogs accumulate, since unfortunately this pit has served and continues to serve as a final destination for these animals. A reasonable amount of ceiling and wall concretion can be seen in this area of the cavity. On the east side there is a gallery of approximately 5 m in length with a small chamber at the end filled with bones.

To continue the visit, we have to climb 3 m up the wall to reach the window that is clearly visible on the west side of the pit. We find ourselves in the Galeria de ses Egagròpiles (Pellet Gallery), the home and dining area of a nocturnal raptor, at least during the time of our visit, where we found a large quantity of owl pellets scattered throughout this gallery of 2 x 10 m, nearly flat and well decorated, which anticipates what we will encounter throughout the rest of the cave.

At the end of the gallery, after passing by two beautiful columns, a step of a couple of meters brings us to the Sala des Tall (Cut Chamber), measuring 4 x 3 x 5.5 m, which like the Pellet Gallery is beautifully decorated with concretions, especially in the ceiling area where lance-shaped stalactites abound, some reaching 2 m in length. At the back of the chamber, a window allows us to look into the Sala de sa Finestra (Window Chamber). This is a chamber of 4.5 x 4 x 7 m which, following the pattern of the cave, abounds in all types of formations. At the bottom of the chamber, a narrow passage of approximately 0.60 x 0.35 m allows us to access the Saleta dets Ossets (Small Bones Chamber). After overcoming a small ramp, this is a small chamber of 8 x 4 x 4 m that functions as a sink, with an almost completely flat floor covered in red clay.

On the walls of the chamber, marks left by the water level that was reached at some point can be observed. In a corner of the chamber, hundreds of rodent bones accumulate. This point marks the -41 m level, being the deepest point of the cavity.
